hooks.prompt:
Check if the plan file already contains '<!-- metis: done -->'. If it does, Metis already ran — proceed with EnterPlanMode.

If the marker is NOT present, run ora:Metis before planning. Spawn Agent(subagent_type='ora:Metis') with the user's full request text. Wait for its analysis. Once Metis returns, write its directives (Intent Classification, Pre-Analysis Findings, Directives for Planner) into the top of the plan file, then append '<!-- metis: done -->'.

Next, check if Metis returned any 'Questions for User'. If it did, present them to the user via AskUserQuestion (organized by priority — most blocking first). Once the user answers, write their decisions into the plan file under a 'Key Decisions' section. Then proceed with EnterPlanMode and use the directives + decisions to shape your plan.

If this plan has 2 or more implementation steps, check if the plan file contains '<!-- momus: okay -->' (case-insensitive). If present, proceed with ExitPlanMode. If NOT present, validate with ora:Momus — it catches blocking issues like missing files, impossible tasks, and contradictions. Spawn Agent(subagent_type='ora:Momus') with the full plan text and wait for its verdict. If [REJECT], fix cited issues and re-validate. If [OKAY], append '<!-- momus: okay -->' to the plan file, then proceed with ExitPlanMode. If the plan has only 1 step, proceed with ExitPlanMode directly.

Check if the plan file contains '<!-- atlas: dispatched -->'. If present, proceed with implementation directly.

If NOT present, and the plan has any code implementation tasks (creating files, modifying code, refactoring, adding features), spawn Agent(subagent_type='ora:Atlas') with the full plan text. Atlas will return a wave dispatch plan that assigns tasks to the right agents (ora:Hephaestus for code work, ora:Ariadne for exploration, ora:Clio for external research) and groups them into execution waves. Append the wave dispatch output to the plan file, then append '<!-- atlas: dispatched -->'. Implement by following the wave dispatch — all tasks within a wave as parallel Agent calls in one message.

Only skip Atlas if the plan is purely research/exploration with no code changes.
